ITEM 1A. RISK FACTORS

Tesla, Inc. 10-K Annual Report — Fiscal Year 2024
United States Securities and Exchange Commission

ITEM 1. BUSINESS

Tesla, Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, leases, and sells electric vehicles, energy generation and storage systems, and related products and services. The Company also offers vehicle service, charging infrastructure, and full self-driving capability software. Tesla was incorporated in Delaware in July 2003.

ITEM 1A. ADDITIONAL RISK FACTORS

Cybersecurity Risk: The Company's products, services, and business operations are subject to cybersecurity threats including malware, ransomware, and sophisticated state-sponsored attacks that could disrupt operations or expose sensitive customer data.

ITEM 1A. RISK FACTORS

AI and Competitive Risk: The Company has made substantial investments in artificial intelligence, including its partnership with OpenAI and integration of Copilot features across products. There is no assurance these investments will generate expected returns. Competitors including Google, Amazon, Meta, and Apple are also investing heavily in AI capabilities. Fa

SERVICES AND OTHER

Services and Other revenues were $10.1 billion in fiscal 2024, compared to $8.3 billion in fiscal 2023. This segment includes non-warranty after-sales vehicle services, body shop and parts, paid Supercharging, retail merchandise, and insurance.
